mapping.a_element: Batalin-Vilkovisky path-integral / BV quantization of the QFT
mapping.b_element: differential Gerstenhaber-Batalin-Vilkovisky (dGBV) algebra of the Barannikov-Kontsevich construction

mapping.a_element: moduli space M parametrizing the flat family of QFTs
mapping.b_element: the Frobenius manifold (deformation/unfolding space of the theory)

mapping.a_element: tangent space at the initial QFT = space of all observables
mapping.b_element: tangent space of the Frobenius manifold carrying the commutative product (cohomology of the dGBV algebra)

mapping.a_element: flat quantum connection on the tangent bundle over M
mapping.b_element: the flat structure / flat (Dubrovin-type) connection of the Frobenius manifold

mapping.a_element: flat connection used to determine all correlation functions of the family
mapping.b_element: the potential/prepotential of the Frobenius manifold encoding its structure constants (e.g. Gromov-Witten correlators in Quantum Cohomology)

mapping.a_element: products of quantum observables are not observables, forcing the whole family
mapping.b_element: the product structure deforming over the Frobenius manifold rather than being fixed pointwise

mapping.a_element: tensor products of d-algebras under the universal quantization machine
mapping.b_element: tensor products of Frobenius manifolds (Kunneth in QC, direct sum of singularities, tensor product of dGBV algebras)

brief_justification: Both papers construct a flat structure (a flat connection over a moduli/deformation space whose flat coordinates encode correlation functions) out of a Batalin-Vilkovisky algebra: paper A's BV path integral yields a family of QFTs over M with a flat quantum connection determining all correlators, which is exactly paper B's Frobenius-manifold flat structure produced from a differential GBV algebra in the Barannikov-Kontsevich construction, matching down to the parallel notion of tensor products.
